name: Game of Thrones
description: Westeros, Essos, and the battle for the Iron Throne

questions.q: What is the name of Arya Stark's sword?

source.notes: Gift from her half-brother Jon Snow. Small enough to use like a fencing foil.

questions.q: Which actor plays Tyrion Lannister on HBO's 'Game of Thrones'?

source.notes: Won 4 Emmy Awards for the role. Youngest Lannister child.

questions.q: What is the motto of House Stark?

source.notes: House words of the Starks of Winterfell. A warning, not a boast.

questions.q: What are the names of Daenerys Targaryen's three dragons?

source.notes: Named after Khal Drogo, her brother Rhaegar, and brother Viserys.

questions.q: Which nomadic warrior culture is known as 'the horselords'?

source.notes: Led by Khals. Daenerys marries Khal Drogo in Season 1.

questions.q: In the notorious 'Red Wedding,' which Stark was killed along with his mother and pregnant wife?

source.notes: Season 3, Episode 9. At the wedding of Edmure Tully to Roslin Frey.

questions.q: Who is the author of the 'A Song of Ice and Fire' book series that the show is based on?

source.notes: First book 'A Game of Thrones' published 1996. Series still unfinished.

questions.q: What is the capital city of the Seven Kingdoms, home of the Iron Throne?

source.notes: Built by Aegon I Targaryen on the east coast of Westeros.

questions.q: What is the name of the massive ice wall that defends the northern border of Westeros?

source.notes: 700 feet tall. Built 8,000 years ago, legendarily with magic.

questions.q: Which major character is executed by beheading in season 1, shocking audiences?

source.notes: Ordered by King Joffrey. Played by Sean Bean, who famously dies in many films.

questions.q: In the series, who turns out to be Jon Snow's biological mother?

source.notes: Revealed in Season 6. Father is Prince Rhaegar Targaryen, making Jon a legitimate heir.

questions.q: Hodor, the gentle giant of Winterfell, can only say 'Hodor,' which is revealed to be a contraction of what phrase?

source.notes: Revealed in 'The Door' (Season 6, Episode 5). Real name: Wylis.

questions.q: Who kills King Joffrey at his own wedding feast?

source.notes: Poisoned wine ('The Strangler'). Scheme by Olenna and Littlefinger.

questions.q: Who kills Tywin Lannister with a crossbow while Tywin sits on the toilet?

source.notes: Season 4 finale. Tyrion escapes King's Landing after doing so.

questions.q: The unofficial Lannister motto is 'A Lannister always pays his...' what?

source.notes: Official motto is 'Hear Me Roar!' but the 'debts' line is far more quoted.

questions.q: The Iron Throne is made from approximately how many swords, according to legend?

source.notes: Forged from swords of surrendered enemies, melted by dragonfire.

questions.q: Which noble lady is Brienne of Tarth secretly in love with, dubbed 'Renly's knight'?

source.notes: Renly knights her. She later develops feelings for Jaime Lannister.

questions.q: Which Stark child becomes the Three-Eyed Raven?

source.notes: Brandon Stark, played by Isaac Hempstead Wright. Becomes 'Bran the Broken' in the finale.

questions.q: The HBO series 'Game of Thrones' ran from 2011 to what year?

source.notes: 8 seasons, 73 episodes. Final season was widely criticized.

questions.q: What is the name of the order of men sworn to guard the Wall?

source.notes: Take vows for life. 'Night gathers, and now my watch begins.'

questions.q: What is the name of Jon Snow's direwolf?

source.notes: Albino direwolf. Jon's is the only direwolf named for a color (rather than a historical figure).

questions.q: Which sellsword becomes Tyrion Lannister's loyal (and self-serving) bodyguard and companion?

source.notes: Played by Jerome Flynn. Eventually becomes Lord of Highgarden and Master of Coin.
